_about: Topic-level bibliographic pointer index into the secondary literature on the Sanskrit grammatical tradition, used by commentary_corpus.py to attach a 'who studied this' reference layer to a commentator frame. NOT full text (rights) — pointers only. Named-section/topic pointers are safe to cite; any page range carries "pages":"verify" and MUST be confirmed against the physical copy before it enters the dissertation. Authored for H414 (SanskritGrammar), Opus 4.8 (claude-opus-4-8), 09-07-2026.

_works.cardona1997: Cardona, George (1997). Pāṇini: A Survey of Research. Delhi: Motilal Banarsidass (reprint of the 1976 Mouton edition). A section-numbered research survey; cited here by named division.
_works.staal1985: Staal, J. F. (ed.) (1985 [1972]). A Reader on Sanskrit Grammarians. Delhi: Motilal Banarsidass (reprint of MIT Press 1972). An anthology of annotated excerpts from and studies of the grammarians; cited here by the contributor/excerpt.
_works.scharfe1977: Scharfe, Hartmut (1977). Grammatical Literature. A History of Indian Literature, Vol. V, Fasc. 2. Wiesbaden: Harrassowitz. A chapter survey of the vyākaraṇa literature; cited here by chapter/section topic.

topics.cardona1997: on the Dhātupāṭha and the status of the verbal root (dhātu) in Pāṇinian analysis
topics.staal1985: excerpts on the analysis of verbal forms and the notion of dhātu (see the Patañjali and Kātyāyana selections)
topics.scharfe1977: ch. on Pāṇini — the derivational base and the Dhātupāṭha as an ancillary list
topics.frame_note: The verbal root (dhātu) is a derivational primitive of Pāṇinian grammar (sū. 1.3.1 bhūvādayo dhātavaḥ, 3.1.32 sanādyantā dhātavaḥ); the Dhātupāṭha enumerates the primary roots, and the commentators debate its authorship, completeness, and the semantic glosses (artha) attached to each root.

topics.cardona1997: the section devoted to the Dhātupāṭha (authorship, recensions, its relation to the Aṣṭādhyāyī, the anubandhas and the artha-glosses)
topics.staal1985: the historical discussion of the ancillary texts (khila-pāṭhas)
topics.scharfe1977: the Dhātupāṭha among Pāṇini's ancillary lists; later recensions and Kṣīrasvāmin / Sāyaṇa / Maitreyarakṣita commentaries
topics.frame_note: The Dhātupāṭha is not part of the Aṣṭādhyāyī proper but an ancillary root-list; the tradition questions how much of the received list is Pāṇini's and how much accreted (Böhtlingk's XIX-c. suspicion of interpolation stands in this line — RWS_REPORT.md §6, entry lidova at ll. 511).

topics.cardona1997: the section on Kātyāyana and the vārttikas (their method: acceptance, restriction, supplementation and refutation of Pāṇini's sūtras)
topics.staal1985: the Kātyāyana selections illustrating the vārttika as a critical gloss on the sūtra
topics.scharfe1977: ch. on Kātyāyana — the vārttika technique and its relative chronology after Pāṇini
topics.frame_note: A vārttika is a critical annotation on a sūtra; Kātyāyana characteristically flags what is left unstated (anukta), over-stated, or wrongly stated — the canonical formula being that a vārttika comments on 'ukta, anukta, durukta'. This is the traditional mechanism by which the commentary CONTESTS or EXTENDS Pāṇini rather than merely paraphrasing him.

topics.cardona1997: the section on Patañjali and the Mahābhāṣya (its dialogic method, the adjudication between sūtra and vārttika, iṣṭi and the appeal to usage)
topics.staal1985: the Patañjali excerpts (incl. the Paspaśāhnika on the purpose of grammar and śiṣṭa authority)
topics.scharfe1977: ch. on Patañjali — the Mahābhāṣya as a running debate that arbitrates Kātyāyana's objections against Pāṇini
topics.frame_note: Patañjali's Mahābhāṣya stages the sūtra and vārttika against each other as a dialogue (pūrvapakṣa / siddhānta); the school's settled view (siddhānta) on a root or rule is what the bhāṣya finally endorses — so the bhāṣya is the layer that ADJUDICATES which classification the tradition stands behind.

topics.cardona1997: the discussion of the criteria of correctness (śiṣṭa usage, the aim of grammar) in the Paspaśā and Cardona's treatment of Pāṇini's methodology
topics.staal1985: the Paspaśāhnika excerpt (Patañjali on who the śiṣṭa is and why their usage is authoritative)
topics.scharfe1977: the discussion of the purpose of grammar and the status of correct (sādhu) speech
topics.frame_note: The authority behind a classification is, in the tradition's own terms, the usage of the śiṣṭas (the learned) as codified by Pāṇini and defended by Patañjali — not an individual's stipulation. This is the 'какие авторитеты стояли за классификациями' answer in Pāṇinian terms.

topics.cardona1997: the treatment of the guṇa/vṛddhi apophony rules and the saṃjñā-sūtras that open the Aṣṭādhyāyī
topics.staal1985: selections illustrating the technical saṃjñā apparatus
topics.scharfe1977: the account of Pāṇini's metalinguistic apparatus (saṃjñā, paribhāṣā)
topics.frame_note: vṛddhi (sū. 1.1.1 vṛddhir ādaic) and guṇa (sū. 1.1.2 adeṅ guṇaḥ) are defined saṃjñās applied across derivation; the commentators debate the scope of tapara-karaṇa and how the definitions restrict the vowels they name.

topics.cardona1997: the treatment of the ātmanepada/parasmaipada assignment rules (sū. 1.3.12ff.) and the role of the anubandhas of the root
topics.staal1985: selections on the affix and voice apparatus
topics.scharfe1977: the account of the pada assignment and the diacritic (it) marking of roots
topics.frame_note: Whether a root is ātmanepadin, parasmaipadin, or ubhayapadin is fixed by its anubandhas in the Dhātupāṭha (ṅit → ātmanepada, etc.) and by the sū. 1.3.12–1.3.93 block; the commentators treat apparent exceptions via iṣṭi and vārttika.

topics.cardona1997: the account of the it-saṃjñā (anubandha) markers and their elision (sū. 1.3.2–1.3.9), and Cardona's analysis of Pāṇini's metalinguistic devices
topics.staal1985: selections on the it-markers as a metalinguistic coding device
topics.scharfe1977: the description of the anubandha system as diacritic coding on roots and affixes
topics.frame_note: Anubandhas are diacritic markers attached to roots/affixes that trigger operations then elide (tasya lopaḥ, 1.3.9); they are the tradition's device for packing morphosyntactic behaviour onto a citation form. (Zaхарьин 2007 is cited for this in the dissertation — RWS_REPORT.md §6, panini-traditional at l. 347: attribute the reading to Patañjali/Kātyāyana, not only to the modern source.)

topics.cardona1997: the treatment of the ten gaṇas and the vikaraṇa affixes (sū. 3.1.68ff.) organizing the Dhātupāṭha
topics.staal1985: selections on the derivation of finite verb forms
topics.scharfe1977: the account of the Dhātupāṭha's arrangement into gaṇas and the present-class vikaraṇas
topics.frame_note: The Dhātupāṭha groups roots into ten gaṇas keyed to their present-stem vikaraṇa (bhvādi, adādi, juhotyādi, divādi, svādi, tudādi, rudhādi, tanādi, kryādi, curādi); the gaṇa membership, not the root's phonology alone, drives the conjugation.

topics.cardona1997: Pāṇini's handling of Vedic (chandas) vs. current (bhāṣā) usage and Cardona's discussion of the relative chronology of the layers
topics.staal1985: the Mahābhāṣya excerpts distinguishing chandas and bhāṣā forms
topics.scharfe1977: the treatment of the Vedic/spoken distinction in Pāṇini and the Mahābhāṣya
topics.frame_note: Pāṇini marks specifically-Vedic forms with 'chandasi' and treats bhāṣā as the default; the commentators read the difference as jāti/register within one grammar, not as two historical languages — cf. the Mahābhāṣya on laukika vs. vaidika (RWS_REPORT.md §6, lidova at l. 543: cite a Mahābhāṣya fragment on the bhāṣā/chandas split).

topics.cardona1997: the treatment of accent (svara) rules and the accentual anubandhas of roots
topics.scharfe1977: the account of accent marking in the sūtras and the Dhātupāṭha's accentual anubandhas
topics.frame_note: Root accent is coded by anubandha in the Dhātupāṭha (anudāttet roots take iṭ / ātmanepada tendencies etc.); the phonological accent rules sit mainly in adhyāyas 6.1–6.2 and are heavily worked over in the bhāṣya.

topics.cardona1997: the treatment of the iṭ-augment (sū. 7.2.8ff.) and the seṭ/aniṭ/veṭ tripartition of roots
topics.staal1985: selections on ārdhadhātuka affixation and the iṭ-augment
topics.scharfe1977: the account of the iṭ-augment and its coding on roots
topics.frame_note: The seṭ / aniṭ / veṭ status of a root (whether ārdhadhātuka affixes take the iṭ-augment) is fixed partly by anit-lists (7.2.10 ekāca upadeśe 'nudāttāt) and partly by the root's anudātta anubandha; the dissertation's proposal to mark seṭ-ness with a symbol 'H' is to be linked here to Kuiper's 'abstract i' and the school's debate on i-insertion (RWS_REPORT.md §6, lidova at l. 309).
